project.name: mp4b
project.openP1: SUBMISSION-READY (v014). Moderate flow-preserving polish over v013 (an LLM review applied selectively - NO restructure, reversal spine + access-gap adjudication kept). Added explicit evaluation after every intervention (CBT value-depends-on-reach; parent-led 'for whom'; guided digital limit = adherence/implementation not efficacy; SSRI when/why-indicated); split dense sentences; hedged two assertions with explicit evidence; sharpened conclusion. 2,065 body words (top of range, matches student habit). 46 refs, 0 ghosts/orphans, 46/46 reconciled to Zotero; reports carried forward (no new factual claims -> paraphrase audit 44/46 verbatim unchanged). Only open item: Woodward 2001 abstract-level (paywalled, acceptable). v013 retained as fallback. [PRIOR v013: full storyline rebuild gated on Elicit Run 6 - reversed evidence-led thesis (therapies kept pace, system/prevention did not). Full storyline rebuild (gated on Elicit Run 6), reconciled + reported. Reversed evidence-led thesis: the therapies HAVE kept pace (routine CBT ~ trial CBT, Wergeland 2021); the failure is the delivery system (access gap: Ball/Abel/Radez/Ghafari) + absent upstream prevention vs rising incidence (prevention paradox: Skinner/Patton). Five-section compounding arc; funnels to parent-led + guided/digital CBT + indicated prevention. DONE: 46/46 refs reconciled to Zotero (fixed Broeren->2012, Radez->2022, Wergeland->2021), 0 ghosts/orphans; full report suite (fqa-report + BQA reference-check + paraphrase-audit); 08 Final synced to v013; 1,917 body words. OPTIONAL POLISH: grow ~150-200 words toward student's habitual ~2,100 (Alex amber); full text for 4 abstract-only sources (Ball/Ginsburg/Ghafari/Bang); add Casey/Sisk DOIs to Zotero; Grummitt in-press pages. [v012: evidence-integrity fix - corrected Goldfield (2026) usage (undergraduate sample was mislabelled 'rare adolescent trial'); Goldfield PDF now full-text, metadata finalised (J Affect Disord 403, 121331), Zotero import + wiki card added. Still open: add 2 DOIs to Zotero for the developmental-brain sources so the library matches the essay: 10.1016/j.neuron.2015.05.020 (Casey 2015) and 10.1093/oso/9780195314373.003.0007 (Sisk & Romeo 2019). Plus the nicety: 1 in-press ref remaining (Grummitt) gets final page numbers when issued (Goldfield + Karawekpanyawong now resolved). [v011: applied v010 FQA tone/format fixes (plain cover, un-numbered headings, child-specific presentations, Figure 1); FQA 92. v010: added Section 2 'Anxiety and the Developing Brain' (Casey/Sisk), renumbered, dropped 2 sentences; FQA 91. DONE v009: intervention-centred rebuild (brief realigned ~70/30); clean submission (boxes stripped); FULL-TEXT Jackie cross-check + BQA complete (34 full-text + 3 image-only = 37/37 on file); Ewart overstatement corrected; systematic essay-vs-Zotero diff applied -> essay reference list now matches the authoritative Zotero/Crossref library with 0 mismatches (fixed Lebowitz->2020 59(3):362-372, Sigurvinsdottir->2020 74(3):168-180, Park->2025 vol13, Sebastian->5(10):1266-1268, +earlier Zhou/Grist/Teesson/Csirmaz/Kaajalaakso/Baschab). BQA PASS, 0 ghosts/orphans. Zotero file is authoritative + final. v008 retained as fallback.]
